weather.summary: Saturday: 17°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 11°C, light rain

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with fun! Camden Market is having a roaring Spring celebration with animals and crafts, and the Southbank Centre has free creative events, so there's something for everyone despite the rainy forecast on Sunday. Let's get started!

activities.title: Jurassic Jungle at the Brunswick Centre
activities.description: Roaming dinosaurs, including a Brontosaurus and dino babies. Live Dino Shows will also be available.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs are always a hit! The roaming dinos will be exciting and the shows provide a structured activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Russell Square.  Walk 5 minutes.
transport.tip: Check the Brunswick Centre website for specific dates and times of the Dino Shows.

activities.title: Spitalfields Market Easter Activities
activities.description: Free Easter Art Workshops for all ages with LuluJoelle.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative and engaging, perfect for little hands and imaginations.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street. Walk 5 minutes.
transport.tip: Check Spitalfields Market website for workshop times.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Interactive museum designed for children, with areas for design, imagination, and play. Sensory explosion for babies and toddlers in Mini Museum.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for young children with lots of hands-on exhibits and sensory experiences. Mini Museum is perfect for 3-year-olds.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the bus 26, 55 or 242 to Bethnal Green Station.  Walk 2 minutes.
transport.tip: Check the Young V&A website for classes such as Sound Explorers and Mini Play.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A real working farm in the heart of Hackney, with animals to see and interact with.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals! A great opportunity to see farm animals up close and learn about them. Small and manageable for little legs.

transport.fromHackney: Walk, cycle or take the bus 26, 55 or 48 to Hackney Road.
transport.tip: Check the farm's website for special events.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Interactive story worlds and a story garden playground bring children's books to life.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive play space and changing pop-up exhibits based on children's books. A great place to spark imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as it can get busy.

activities.title: The Everywhere Bear
activities.description: A charming puppet show about a little bear's adventures.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppet shows are engaging for young children, and the Little Angel Theatre is renowned for its quality productions.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 30 or 277 to Islington Green, then walk 5 mins.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Camden Market Easter Weekender
activities.description: Roar into Spring! With Blossom the Lion, a circus acrobat, funny farmers, an Easter trail, Bonnets & Bunnies workshop, spring flower planting, face painting, and a Cheeky Meeky Family Rave.
activities.whyItWorks: A variety of activities to keep a 3-year-old entertained, including animals, crafts, and music. The outdoor setting is great for letting off steam.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Camden Road or the bus 29 or 253 to Camden Town.
transport.tip: Some activities may require booking in advance.

activities.title: London Mithraeum Bloomberg SPACE
activities.description: Explore the ruins of an ancient Roman temple. Interactive displays, Roman artefacts and sensory elements make the history fun and accessible for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and sensory displays bring Roman history to life. Exploring real ruins is exciting for kids.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bank Station.
transport.tip: Booking your free, timed ticket before your visit to avoid queues and guarantee entry is recommended.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 5BD
activities.description: Explore the history of planet Earth, with interactive exhibits perfect for children. See dinosaurs, learn about animals, and discover the wonders of the natural world.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs! Interactive exhibits are engaging for young children. Plenty of space to move around.

transport.fromHackney: Take the District Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit the website for special events and family activities during the Easter holidays.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Discover how the world works with hands-on exhibits. The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for younger children.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits are great for learning and exploration. The Garden is specifically designed for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the District Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and shows.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.address: North End Way, Golders Green, London NW3 7HE
activities.description: A small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare species of birds and animals. Wallabies, lemurs, Scottish wildcat and more!
activities.whyItWorks: Free and fun! A great place to see animals without the crowds of a larger zoo.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Gospel Oak, then the Overground to Hampstead Heath. Walk 20 minutes or take the 210 bus.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Crystal Palace Park Dinosaurs
activities.description: Explore the dinosaur sculptures in Crystal Palace Park and climb on the wooden carved dinosaurs in the play area.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs! A fun and free outdoor activity. The playground is great for burning energy.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Overground to Crystal Palace.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic and make a day of it!

activities.title: Walthamstow Wetlands - The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Discover illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats, identify the letter on each one and complete the secret word in the activity booklet.
activities.whyItWorks: An interactive trail that combines nature and learning. The illustrated animals are appealing for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria Line to Tottenham Hale, then walk 15 minutes.
transport.tip: Check the Walthamstow Wetlands website for trail details and prices.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.address: Hampton Court, East Molesey, Surrey KT8 9AU
activities.description: Follow the trail and match the names of influential characters to the red ribbons adorning the Lindt Gold Bunny statues scattered around the palace grounds, then collect a small chocolate prize at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Egg hunt with a chocolate prize! Exploring the palace grounds is a fun way to get exercise.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then a train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as it can get busy.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: A riverside hub of arts, fun and food with kids' concerts, festivals, creative workshops, hands-on installations and space to scoot or picnic.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of free events and activities for children, plus space to run around. A great place to spend a day.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 55 to Waterloo Station.
transport.tip: Check the website for a full list of family events.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: A full day of adventure with animals, play areas, and an egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Greenford, then the E6 bus to Hobbledown Heath.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Battersea Power Station - The Elephant Trail
activities.description: A free art trail supporting Elephant Family with discounts, prizes and fun to be found throughout the trail. Download the app and collect every Elephant to reveal an exclusive offer, plus competitions at milestones!
activities.whyItWorks: The Elephant Trail is a fun, engaging way to explore Battersea Power Station with a 3-year-old. Combining art, exploration, and prizes can make for an exciting and memorable outing.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria Line to Vauxhall, then the train to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Download the app before you go.

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: An immensely successful reimagining of Disney's animation continues to captivate audiences.
activities.whyItWorks: Visually stunning and with familiar songs, this show is a classic for a reason.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Covent Garden.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Paddington the Musical
activities.description: Pack your marmalade sandwiches and welcome the world's most beloved bear to the stage!
activities.whyItWorks: A charming musical about a beloved character. Great for introducing young children to theatre.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Covent Garden, then walk 10 mins.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: War Horse
activities.description: A breathtaking fusion of drama and innovative puppetry based on Michael Morpurgo's novel.
activities.whyItWorks: The puppetry is amazing! This show is a moving and unforgettable experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 55 to Waterloo Station, then walk 10 minutes.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Mighty Hoopla
activities.description: Dance the weekend away at this festival packed with big-name acts.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun and energetic festival with music and activities for all ages. A great way to enjoy the outdoors.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Brockwell Park.
transport.tip: Check website for details and ticket information.

activities.title: Regent's Park Open Air Theatre
activities.address: Inner Circle, Regent's Park, London NW1 4NU
activities.description: Enjoy world-class theatre in a stunning outdoor setting all summer long.
activities.whyItWorks: A unique theatre experience in a beautiful park. Great for a summer evening.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 30 to Baker Street, then walk 10 minutes.
transport.tip: Check website for schedule and ticket information.

activities.title: Imagine Children's Festival
activities.times: Various, check website nearer the time
activities.description: The UK's leading arts festival for children aged 0–11 years and their grown-ups. The festival's multi-artform programme presents music, storytelling, dance, crafts, comedy and more with dozens of events on the programme – many of them free to attend!
activities.whyItWorks: A huge range of activities for children of all ages, with many free options. A great way to spend a day.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 55 to Waterloo Station.
transport.tip: Study the programme beforehand and make bookings so you don't miss out. Shows, performances and activities are listed according to the ages for which they are most appropriate.
